lines.text: WFO 62-0
in the matter. [REDACTED] was questioned concerning the position that the Bureau
of Aeronautics had taken and he remarked that he did not believe that this
was pertinent. After further questioning, he subsequently stated that the b7c
Bureau of Aeronautics was of the belief that this was a matter for ONI.
[REDACTED] stated that he "unofficially" became interested in the
matter and at the invitation of [REDACTED] he went to [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] where he spent 7/24,25,26/54 in the residence of [REDACTED] b7c
[REDACTED] stated that he informed Admiral SOUCEK, his superior in the Bureau
of Aeronautics, that he was going to visit [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] stated that inasmuch as [REDACTED] had received no response
from the Government concerning this matter, he began reading books on b7c
flying saucers. [REDACTED] advised that during the time that he was a house
guest of [REDACTED] there was also present [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] who was also interested in flying saucers.
[REDACTED] stated that [REDACTED] was at [REDACTED] residence with his family and was
there in an unofficial capacity.
[REDACTED] advised that during his stay, he and [REDACTED] had various
interviews with [REDACTED] and had had contact with the "outer space" through
[REDACTED] During the interview with [REDACTED] and the contact with "outer
space," [REDACTED] stated that they sat in a group and he observed [REDACTED]
writing messages that she was receiving from someone in "outer space."
According to [REDACTED] informed him that the people from "outer space" b7c
could use her eyes and ears to see and hear. According to [REDACTED] this is
accomplished by the use of a mechanical device and all conversations are
recorded by the people in "outer space." [REDACTED] indicated as an example
as to how messages were relayed, he stated that he asked [REDACTED] a
question which she was to relay to the people in "outer space" and before
she had time to relay the question, she began writing the answer down to
his question on paper.
[REDACTED] stated that [REDACTED] informed him that whenever she was to
have contact with the people in "outer space," she would get a buzzing
sound in her left ear to indicate that they were "on the line." [REDACTED]
stated that [REDACTED] complained to the people in "outer space" that
this buzzing sound gets very annoying and painful to her ear. She also b7c
stated that the messages had been coming since May 27, 1954, and would come
at all times of the night and consequently she was losing a great deal of
sleep. According to [REDACTED] arranged a schedule with the people
in "outer space" so that the messages would not interfere with her sleep.
[REDACTED] advised that a schedule was arranged between [REDACTED] and the people
in "outer space" that she would receive messages on the following schedule:
8:00 in the morning, 12 noon, 6:00 in the evening, every day of the week.
